name: Decathlon Italia
regions: national (145 stores incl. Milano Roma Torino Napoli Palermo Bologna Firenze and most regional capitals)
best_contact_role: Servizio Clienti / Marketing (Rossella Ruggeri, Marketing and Communication Director)
why_target: Largest sporting-goods chain in Italy with 145 stores; dominant retailer of the WC2026 Adidas Trionda match ball and adidas/Nike/Puma replica jerseys; Italian fans buy other-nation jerseys heavily during the World Cup as observers; brand-identity refresh under new Italian CEO Fulvio Matteoni positions partnership stories well
notes: HQ Decathlon Italia Srl, Viale Valassina 268, 20851 Lissone (MB). Parent Decathlon SA (Lille, France). CEO Italia Fulvio Matteoni. PEC decathlonitalia@pec.it. Pattern firstname.lastname@decathlon.com (unverified for individuals).

name: Cisalfa Sport
regions: national (Lazio HQ but 150+ stores across 18 regions incl. Lombardia, Veneto, Emilia-Romagna, Toscana, Campania, Sicilia, Sardegna)
why_target: Italy's largest sporting-goods retailer by Italian ownership; over 240 stores including the new Intersport Superstore format; one yes here unlocks national WC2026 coverage on top of continuing Serie A season cross-promo; group CEO Carlo Cisalfa
notes: Cisalfa Group HQ Via Lega Lombarda 1, 24035 Curno (BG). Parent Cisalfa Sport SpA. Owns Intersport Italia, Tecnosport 2.0, Endeka Sport 2023, plus Sport Voswinkel and SportScheck in Germany. Press inbox media@cisalfagroup.com. HR Director Cristian Arcari. Email pattern lastname.f@cisalfasport.it.

name: Intersport Italia
regions: national (250+ affiliated stores across 120 retail partners incl. Longoni Sport, DF Sport Specialist, Maxi Sport)
why_target: Buying-group co-op of 120 independent retailers running 250+ stores; under the Cisalfa Group umbrella since 2022; member stores have local marketing freedom so a head-office green light cascades to every member's window for WC2026
notes: HQ Bologna; warehouse outsourced to Snatt Logistica (Castelnovo Sotto). Subsidiary of ICC Intersport International Corp (Switzerland). Italian operation rolled into Cisalfa Group portfolio.

name: Maxi Sport
regions: Lombardia (Lissone flagship, Como, Trezzano sul Naviglio, Sesto San Giovanni, Merate) and Brescia
why_target: Long-standing Italian sporting-goods chain anchored by the 2500 sqm Lissone flagship; deep football category incl. boots
notes: balls
column11: futsal; member of Intersport Italia under Cisalfa Group; high foot traffic from Monza-Brianza catchment that includes many Inter and Milan tifosi
column12: Maxi Sport SpA, Strada Nuova Valassina 346, 20851 Lissone (MB). Intersport-affiliated. Customer service hours Lun 14:30-19:30, Mar-Sab 9:30-19:30, Dom 10:00-19:30.

name: Longoni Sport
regions: Lombardia + Piemonte + Liguria (Gallarate, Milano, Cinisello Balsamo, Cogoleto and more)
why_target: Historic Lombardy sports chain founded by the Longoni family; acquired by Cisalfa Sport in 2022 and partially rebranded under Intersport; carries Nike / adidas / Puma football boots and Serie A licensed apparel; strong Varese / Milano nord catchment
notes: HQ Via Statale 15, 23890 Barzago (LC). Now operating as a Cisalfa Sport Spa filiale; corporate route should go through Cisalfa Group partnership desk.

name: DF Sport Specialist
regions: Lombardia + Emilia-Romagna + Canton Ticino (Switzerland), incl. Bevera di Sirtori flagship 'Palazzo dello Sport' (Lissone) at 3500 sqm
why_target: Family-owned northern-Italian chain of 18 large-format stores (1200-3000 sqm); Intersport-affiliated; carries full football boots range from adidas/Nike/Puma/New Balance/Mizuno; rich kits/balls/teamwear catalogue; deeply embedded in Lecco/Como football community
notes: HQ DF Sport Specialist SpA, Via Stabilimento Figliodoni 14, Barzano (LC). Member of Intersport Italia (Cisalfa Group). Customer-service phone 039 9530230. Email amm388@df-sportspecialist.it is the published assistance address.

name: Sportland
regions: Lombardia, Veneto, Emilia-Romagna + selected northern-Italy regions; flagship at Italmark centre Brescia
why_target: Specialist sporting-goods chain of 18 stores owned by Italmark Group (130 retail points across food
notes: electronics
phone: perfumery and DIY); member of Sport 2000 buying co-op; concept stores for running and team sports; foot traffic baked in via Italmark food shoppers
column11: Sportland Srl, c/o Italmark Centre Brescia. Parent Italmark Group (founded 1965). Sport 2000 affiliate. Smaller per-store budget but easier internal sign-off than the Cisalfa group.

name: Universo Sport
regions: Toscana (Firenze, Scandicci, Arezzo), Lazio, Lombardia, Veneto, Emilia-Romagna
why_target: Founded 1997 (Scandicci HQ) with 270 employees and 20 outlets across Tuscany and four other regions; carries football boots and lifestyle football apparel; Toscana presence overlaps with Fiorentina tifosi and Empoli/Pisa Serie A markets
notes: Universo Sport SpA, headquartered in Scandicci (FI). Strong Florence-region foothold complements the Fiorentina retail channel.

name: Nencini Sport
regions: Toscana (Calenzano flagship, Firenze, Livorno, Montecatini, Pistoia, Terranuova Bracciolini)
why_target: Founded 1985; biggest sports retailer in Florence with around 10000 sqm of retail space across multiple Tuscan super-stores; runs one of the earliest Italian sports e-commerce platforms; rich Fiorentina catchment and strong school/club channel
notes: Nencini Sport SpA, Via Salvanti 45, 50041 Calenzano (FI). Famous for ski and outdoor but also stocks Serie A and national-team replica kits.

name: JD Sports Italia
regions: national (Milano Centrale, Milano CityLife, Roma Termini, Torino, Bologna, Modena, Napoli, Palermo, Verona, Vicenza, Sassari, Taranto, Pescara, Novara and more)
why_target: UK sneaker giant with strong WC2026 football boot inventory (Nike
notes: adidas
phone: Puma) and Serie A + national-team replica kits; mall and station foot traffic; Italian Serie A continuing-season cross-promo drives boots into checkout
column11: JD Sports Fashion Srl, Via Montenapoleone 29, 20121 Milano. Parent JD Sports Fashion Plc (LSE-listed, Bury UK). Escalation customerchampions@jdplc.com (unverified for Italy).

name: Foot Locker Italia
regions: national (mall and high-street)
best_contact_email: via Help Center contact form on footlocker.it
why_target: Global sneaker chain; carries WC2026 Adidas Trionda-themed lifestyle drops and a range of national-team replica jerseys; sneaker-culture overlap with Italian football fashion through Fila/Nike/adidas/Jordan
notes: Foot Locker Italy Srl, Via A. Ponchielli 7, 20129 Milano. Parent Foot Locker Inc (US). No public Italy marketing email; submit via Help Center.

name: Snipes Italia
regions: national (Milano CityLife and Corso Buenos Aires, Roma Termini, Roma Est and more)
why_target: German sneaker-streetwear chain; over 300 European stores including a fast-growing Italian footprint; younger 18-30 demographic that overlaps with Italian football social media engagement; carries football-lifestyle Nike/adidas/Jordan drops
notes: SNIPES Italia. Parent SNIPES SE (Cologne, Germany), owned by Signa Sports United / now JD Sports group. Italian retail run by GS Retail.

name: Courir Italia
regions: national (Milano, Roma, Forli, Marcianise, Assago, Venezia, Lonato)
why_target: French sneaker chain expanding rapidly into Italian outlet villages and CBD locations; carries the same WC2026 football-lifestyle Nike/adidas drops; outlet positioning fits a promo-pool sweetener
notes: Courir SAS (Paris, France). Italian growth through outlet villages (Castel Romano, Serravalle, etc.) and major CBD.

name: Macron Sports Hub
regions: 170+ Macron Sports Hub stores worldwide, with the densest cluster in Italy (Bologna, Casalecchio di Reno, Rastignano and more)
why_target: Italian sportswear brand of Bologna FC
notes: SS Lazio
column11: Sporting CP and many more; runs 170+ Sports Hub franchise stores worldwide with a deep Italian footprint; ideal partner because Macron-kitted clubs need WC2026 cross-promo during Serie A
column12: Macron SpA, Via Giulio Pastore 57, 40053 Valsamoggia loc. Crespellano (BO). Italian sportswear manufacturer; campus 22000 sqm expanding to 100000 sqm. Founded 1971. Partnerships dept best route.

name: Erreà
regions: Italian network anchored in Parma + 'Erreà Play Parma' and 'Erreà Republic' single-brand stores across northern Italy
why_target: Italian sports manufacturer with HQ near Parma; technical sponsor of Parma Calcio and historically of many Serie A and European football clubs; Republic line targets young consumers (the WC2026 social-share demographic)
notes: Erreà Sport SpA, Via Sacca 5, 43058 San Polo di Torrile (PR). Founded by Angelo Gandolfi (still president). Strong customisation pitch fits store-branded prediction pools.

name: Sports Direct Italia
regions: online only (no physical stores in Italy yet)
best_contact_email: via Customer Services form on sportsdirect.com
best_contact_role: Customer Services / Frasers Group Italy lead
why_target: Frasers Group flagship online sports retailer; ships into Italy with broad Everlast / Slazenger / Karrimor / adidas / Nike / Puma stock incl. Italy and other-nation Serie A kit; pricing-aggressive online competitor to Decathlon
notes: Frasers Group plc (UK). No Italian physical retail yet; Italian customers serviced via sportsdirect.com.

name: Subside Sports
regions: online (UK / EU multi-region site) + Berlin physical store
why_target: Approved international football-shirt retailer with a dedicated Italy / Serie A vertical; full Italy national team range with name and number printing; stocks Serie A B and C jerseys plus other-nation WC2026 kits that Italian fans buy as observers
notes: Subside Sports BV (Netherlands HQ) with Berlin storefront and UK ops. Ships to Italy in multiple languages. Email subsidesports.com pattern firstname@subsidesports.com (unverified).

name: Classic Football Shirts
regions: online (global) + physical stores in Manchester, London, NYC
why_target: World-leading retro and modern football shirt retailer with a deep Italy / Serie A catalogue; vintage Azzurri jerseys cluster around 1990 and 2006 nostalgia which Italian fans will lean into harder while their team is absent from WC2026
notes: Classic Football Shirts Ltd, Manchester UK. Italian shoppers are a top-five market for the site's Serie A and Italy national team verticals.

name: Footballitalia.it
regions: online (Italy national)
why_target: Italian online football specialist retailer; multi-brand boots and replica jersey stock; small-team-decision agility makes them quick to say yes to a no-cost prediction-pool overlay
notes: Italy-based domain. Owner-operator scale, ideal pilot partner for an Italian-language store-branded pool.

name: Foot-Store.it
regions: online (Italy national)
why_target: French-rooted football specialist with a fully localised Italian storefront; goalkeeper and referee verticals as well as boots and balls; published direct customer-service email; quick partner-style turnaround
notes: Operated by Foot-Store / La Boutique du Football SAS (Paris, France). Italian site is the localised storefront for IT customers.

name: Officina dello Sport
regions: Castelfranco Veneto (TV) + national online
why_target: Veneto football specialist with one of the deeper Italian online football catalogues; boots / jerseys / balls / training equipment; WhatsApp-first service signals partner-friendly small business
notes: Officina dello Sport Srl, Via della Borsa 1/E, 31033 Castelfranco Veneto (TV). Phone 0423 371245. WhatsApp +39 329 168 5626. Hours Lun 15:30-19:30, Mar-Sab 10-12:30/15:30-19:30.

name: Pantofola d'Oro
regions: Ascoli Piceno HQ + online + selected wholesale
why_target: Handcrafted Italian football boot heritage brand (Lazzarini cobbler 1886; football boots since 1950s); Ascoli Piceno factory; Italian-made narrative is a perfect WC2026 story angle for a country whose team is absent
notes: Pantofola d'Oro SpA, Ascoli Piceno (AP). Italy's most famous artisan football boot maker. Cross-listed at Sports Direct, Goalinn, Pro:Direct.

name: FIGC Store (Italia National Team)
regions: online + temporary popups around national fixtures
why_target: Official online retailer for the Italy national team (Azzurri) and FIGC merchandise; adidas-supplied match and training kits with custom name and number; emotional retention play for fans navigating a no-Azzurri WC2026
notes: Operated by Fanatics Commerce Italy on behalf of FIGC (Federazione Italiana Giuoco Calcio). The Italy-fans-without-Italy narrative is perfectly placed at this store.

name: Juventus Megastore (Allianz Stadium)
regions: Torino + global online + 9 owned stores incl. Milano and Roma
why_target: Juventus is Italy's most-followed football brand globally (more than 100M social followers) and its retail arm has 9 owned stores plus a global online; cross-promo to Argentinian (Messi nostalgia) and Brazilian shirt buyers who shop Juve too
notes: Operated by Fanatics Italy. Megastore: Via Druento 153, 10151 Torino. Phone +39 055 029 6272. Other Juventus Stores in Milano (Galleria San Federico/Brera) and Roma.

name: AC Milan Store (Casa Milan / San Siro)
regions: Casa Milan, San Siro stadium, Milano centro and selected outlets
why_target: Seven-time European champion with global brand reach; Casa Milan HQ acts as flagship retail and event space; runs Puma kit so a prediction pool dovetails with Puma's WC2026 boot push; Italian Rossoneri audience and global diaspora
notes: AC Milan SpA, Via Aldo Rossi 8, 20149 Milano. Call centre +39 02 8295 8070 (Lun-Sab 9-18). Sponsorship enquiries sponsorshipenquiries@acmilan.com. Events eventi@acmilan.com.

name: Inter Milan Store (Galleria Passarella / San Siro)
regions: San Babila flagship, Castello Via Dante, San Siro stadium
why_target: Inter is reigning Scudetto holder and 2023 UCL finalist with deep international fanbase; flagship San Babila store sits on one of Milano's busiest pedestrian corridors; Nike kit ties into WC2026 Nike boot drops
notes: FC Internazionale Milano SpA. San Babila: Galleria Passarella 2, 20122 Milano (+39 02 7601 6297). Castello: Via Dante 16, 20122 (+39 02 9713 9277). San Siro Gate 14: Piazzale Angelo Moratti, 20151 (+39 02 8219 7350). PEC customerservicefanatics@postecertificate.it.

name: AS Roma Store
regions: Roma (Piazza Colonna, Piazza dei Cinquecento, Via del Corso, Via Arenula, Via Ottaviano)
why_target: Roma carries one of Italy's most passionate domestic followings (Curva Sud); flagship Piazza Colonna store is a top tourist photo-stop; prediction pool tied to other-nation shirts (Brasile
notes: Argentina) works because Roma fans buy them as second jerseys
phone: AS Roma SpA, Roma. Call centre +39 06 8938 6000 (Lun-Ven 9:00-18:30). Multiple Rome storefronts incl. Piazza Colonna 360 and Via del Corso 26/27. Online store operated under Fanatics arrangement.

name: SS Lazio Store (Lazio Style 1900)
regions: Roma (Via di Propaganda, Via Calderini, Roma Est, Valmontone, Ostia, Parco Leonardo)
why_target: Lazio's 'Lazio Style 1900' chain runs 6 owned stores incl. the via di Propaganda flagship off Piazza di Spagna; Mizuno-supplied kit ties into Mizuno's smaller but growing WC2026 boot story; tifosi Biancocelesti deeply engaged year-round
notes: SS Lazio SpA. Via di Propaganda 8A/8B/9/10, 00187 Roma (+39 06 678 1574). Via G. Calderini 66/C, 00196 Roma (+39 06 3254 1745). Roma Est: Via Collatina km 12,800 (+39 06 2251 0795). Parco Leonardo +39 06 6549 9801.

name: SSC Napoli Official Store
regions: Napoli + online
why_target: Napoli is reigning recent Scudetto champion and the only Italian club to be carried into WC2026 hype via its global Maradona-Argentina cultural link; perfect 'watch the world predict' angle since Napoli fans cheer Argentina
notes: SSC Napoli SpA, Via del Maio di Porto 9, 80133 Napoli. Phone (per IG) +39 324 834 5886. Italian-language store with EMP / Fanatics partnership.

name: ACF Fiorentina Store
regions: Firenze (Duomo flagship, Centro Commerciale I Gigli, Coverciano area)
why_target: Viola tifosi have strong year-round engagement; flagship Duomo store at Borgo San Lorenzo 11/R is in the most-visited tourist district in Italy; cross-promo on Conference League continuing campaign and WC2026
notes: ACF Fiorentina SpA. Duomo: Borgo San Lorenzo 11/R, 50123 Firenze (+39 055 029 6174). I Gigli: Campi Bisenzio. Online ops via Fanatics. PEC customerservicefanatics@postecertificate.it.

name: Atalanta BC Store
regions: Bergamo (Bergamo Centro at Viale Papa Giovanni XXIII 30, online)
why_target: Atalanta is the surprise Serie A and Europa League winner with rising international profile; Joma kit; Bergamo Centro flagship is open every day continuously; strong tifosi Nerazzurri fan club network for a localised pool
notes: Atalanta Bergamasca Calcio SpA. Bergamo Centro store: Viale Papa Giovanni XXIII 30. General inbox info@atalanta.it. Joma technical sponsor.

name: Bologna FC 1909 Store
regions: Bologna (Via d'Azeglio 1/E flagship, Stadio Dall'Ara Via dello Sport 6)
best_contact_email: via Customer Care portal on bolognafcstore.com
why_target: Bologna is back in Europe and Macron-kitted; flagship Via d'Azeglio store is on the principal pedestrian corridor; prediction pool fits the club's Champions League continuing-season story
notes: Bologna FC 1909 SpA. Flagship Via d'Azeglio 1/E (+39 055 029 6172). Stadium store Via dello Sport 6 (+39 055 029 6179). PEC customerservicefanatics@postecertificate.it.

name: Torino FC Store (Granata Store)
regions: Torino (Corso Agnelli flagship + online)
why_target: Toro tifosi run an old-school season-ticket community; Corso Agnelli store is a derby-week magnet; smaller club with hungry commercial team easier to close than the European giants
notes: Torino FC SpA. Corso Agnelli 24/A, 10134 Torino. Phone 011 350 713. Original 2006 GranataStore now under torinofcstore.com.

name: Genoa CFC Store
regions: Genova (Via XII Ottobre 43r flagship), online + matchday popups
why_target: Italy's oldest football club (founded 1893) with rising commercial profile under 777 Partners / Dan Sucu and Castore-supplied kit; flagship store opposite De Ferrari metro stop is high-traffic Genova centro
notes: Genoa CFC SpA. Store at Via XII Ottobre 43 rosso, Genova. Castore is technical sponsor and runs the store concierge inbox.

name: Sampdoria Point Store
regions: Genova (Via XX Settembre 252) + online
best_contact_email: via club commercial form on sampdoria.it
why_target: Sampdoria has a fiercely loyal Genovese ultras community even through relegation; Via XX Settembre store is high-traffic shopping street; prediction pool plays well to the under-pressure rebuild narrative
notes: UC Sampdoria SpA. Sampdoria Point at Via XX Settembre 252, 16121 Genova.
